mission.title: Install and Configure Akri on Kubernetes
mission.description: Akri is a Kubernetes Resource Interface for the Edge that allows you to expose heterogeneous leaf devices as resources in a Kubernetes cluster. This is particularly useful for edge computing scenarios where devices like IP cameras and USB devices need to be utilized within Kubernetes workloads.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Akri Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add akri https://project-akri.github.io/akri/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Akri using Helm
steps.description:
Install Akri in a new namespace called 'akri'.
```bash
helm install akri akri/akri --namespace akri --create-namespace --version 0.12.55
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Akri pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n akri
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Akri deployment to ensure optimal performance.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment akri --namespace akri --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Akri release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall akri --namespace akri
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were created by Akri.
```bash
kubectl delete crd akridevices.akri.sh
kubectl delete crd akriresources.akri.sh
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Akri namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Akri namespace to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace akri
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of the Akri deployment for backup.
```bash
kubectl get deployment akri -n akri -o yaml > akri-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Helm chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Akri to the latest version
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Akri installation to the latest version available.
```bash
helm upgrade akri akri/akri --namespace akri --version 0.12.55
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Akri pods are running successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n akri
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue may occur if there are configuration errors. Check the logs of the pods to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n akri
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter errors related to missing CRDs, ensure that they were installed correctly. You can check existing CRDs with:
```bash
kubectl get crd
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits not applied
troubleshooting.description:
If resource limits are not being enforced, verify the deployment configuration with:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment akri -n akri
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Akri will have all pods in the 'akri' namespace running without errors, and the resources will be correctly exposed for use in your Kubernetes cluster. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are in a 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add akri https://project-akri.github.io/akri/
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment akri --namespace akri --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
